
---
about:
  template: broadside
  id: about-block
  image: img/headshot.jpg
  image-shape: rounded
  image-width: 12em
  links:
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/jacob-gardner-10930725b/
    - icon: 0-circle
      text: ORCID
      href: https://orcid.org/0000-0002-9692-2979
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: "mailto:jacgardner@montefiore.org"  
---

::: {#about-block}
:::

I am a General Surgery PGY-1 Resident at Montefiore Medical Center/Albert Einstein College of Medicine. My professional interests revolve around Surgical Oncology, Hepatopancreatobiliary Surgery, and Public/Global Health. Most of my research focuses on cancer epidemiology in developing countries. Test

## Education

Case Western Reserve University School of Medicine | Cleveland, OH

Doctor of Medicine | Jul. 2020 – May. 2025

Case Western Reserve University School of Medicine | Cleveland, OH

Master of Public Health | Jun. 2023 – May. 2024

Duke University | Durham, NC
Bachelor of Science - Neuroscience and Philosophy | Aug. 2013 – May 2017

## Work Experience

Montefiore Medical Center/Albert Einstein College of Medicine, Department of General Surgery | Categorical Resident | Jul. 2025 - Present

Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, National Center for Immunization and Respiratory Diseases | Research Intern | Oct. 2023 - Jun. 2024

Cleveland Clinic Foundation, Lerner Research Institute, Jung Laboratory  | Clinical Research Coordinator | Apr. 2021 - Aug. 2025

Calmette Hospital, National Cancer Centre of Cambodia  | Clinical Research Coordinator | Feb. 2018 - Aug. 2025



On this website, you will find a selection of my academic works, including peer-reviewed journal articles and poster presentations. You will also find news and updates on my current research projects, as well as information on upcoming events and conferences where I will be speaking. Please feel free to [contact me](mailto:jacgardner@montefiore.org) if you have any questions or would like to discuss potential research projects.
